_description: Validated static quadrature intervals for the rho=0.3 Gaussian-copula extension. These scalar enclosures are produced by outward-rounded interval quadrature under the same Abramowitz-Stegun one-sided Phi-bounds and IEEE nextafter rounding discipline used by the Picard step-tube certificate scripts (rho03_production_certificate.py and rho03_fluid_limit_certificate.py). They are referenced by the manuscript via the keys below.

_paper_references.ratio_cuts_rho03: eq:ratio-cuts-rho03 (ratio-cut thresholds at shares m=0.10 and phi)

arithmetic.precision: IEEE 754 double with math.nextafter outward rounding
arithmetic.inverse_normal_bracketing: verified binary search against Abramowitz-Stegun bounds

ratio_cuts_rho03._description: Ratio-cut thresholds r_s(0.3) satisfying Pr(R >= r_s) = s under the Gaussian-copula law.

mx_prefix_value_rho03._description: Expected MX prefix ecological value M_X(m, 0.3) = E[E * 1{R >= r_m(0.3)}] at m=0.10.

mx_affordability_rho03._description: Residual cost function C_R^{(0.3)}(r) = E[A * 1{R >= r}] evaluated at r = r_phi(0.3). Used in the rho=0.3 MX-side Green affordability lemma to establish C_R^{(0.3)}(r_phi) < mu_A/2.
mx_affordability_rho03.notes: C_R^{(0.3)} is strictly decreasing in r; the upper endpoint of the C_R interval uses r_phi_lo (larger integration region, larger integrand) and the lower endpoint uses r_phi_hi. Monte Carlo corroboration at N=10M gives 0.6828 with standard error ~4e-4.
